factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in the trench, power, and pump segments.

evidence.text: The volume of OEC on rent increased 12.4 percent, primarily reflecting improving demand in many of our core markets. In particular, we saw improvement in our trench, power and pump segment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key segments, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: Pricing power is being challenged, with rental rates decreasing.

evidence.text: The decreased rental rates reflected the impact of the NES acquisition and pressure from Canada and the impact of recent industry fleet expansion.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is being challenged due to decreasing rental rates and industry pressures.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly trench, power, and pump.

evidence.text: The improvement in the Pump Solutions region primarily reflected growth in revenue from i) upstream oil and gas customers, and ii) construction and mining customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, which is a positive development.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by increased demand and volume.

evidence.text: Equipment rental revenue increased 9.1 percent as compared to the same period in 2016, primarily reflecting a 12.4 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by increased demand and volume.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased demand and customer activity.

evidence.text: The volume of OEC on rent increased 12.4 percent in our trench, power and pump segment, primarily due to continued strength in our Trench Safety and Power and HVAC regions, and improved performance in our Pump Solutions region.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, reflecting increased demand and customer activity.

factors.summary: End markets such as construction, industrial, and oil and gas are growing fastest.

evidence.text: The improvement in the Pump Solutions region primarily reflected growth in revenue from i) upstream oil and gas customers, which have experienced significant volatility in recent years, and ii) construction and mining customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: End markets such as construction, industrial, and oil and gas are growing fastest, indicating strong momentum.

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure, with EBITDA margins decreasing due to increased delivery costs and compensation expenses.

evidence.text: The decrease in the EBITDA margin primarily reflects i) a slight decrease in the margins, excluding depreciation, from equipment rentals, ii) increased selling, general and administrative ('SG&A') compensation costs, including stock compensation costs, largely due to the impact of the NES acquisition discussed in note 2 to the condensed consolidated financial statements, increased revenue, improved profitability, and increases in our stock price and in the volume of stock awards, and iii) increased merger related costs and restructuring charges primarily associated with the NES acquisition.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are under pressure due to decreased EBITDA margins and increased costs.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives, such as Lean management techniques, are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: We achieved the anticipated run rate savings from the Lean initiatives in 2016 and expect to continue to generate savings from these initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings, indicating effective cost management.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving through Lean management techniques and continuous improvement processes.

evidence.text: The implementation of 'Lean' management techniques, including kaizen processes focused on continuous improvement. We have trained over 3,100 employees, over 70 percent of our district managers and approximately 55 percent of our branch managers on the Lean kaizen process. We continue to implement this program across our branch network, with the objectives of: reducing the cycle time associated with renting our equipment to customers; improving invoice accuracy and service quality; reducing the elapsed time for equipment pickup and delivery; and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of our repair and maintenance operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The implementation of Lean management techniques and continuous improvement processes indicates significant operational efficiency improvements.

factors.summary: Management is confident in their strategy, with a focus on disciplined execution and profitable growth.

evidence.text: In 2017, we expect to continue our disciplined focus on increasing our profitability and return on invested capital.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's focus on disciplined execution and increasing profitability reflects confidence in their strategy.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on expanding product offerings and improving operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Our strategy calls for: The continued expansion of our trench, power and pump footprint, as well as our tools offering, and the cross-selling of these services throughout our network. We believe that the expansion of our trench, power and pump business, as well as our tools offering, will further position United Rentals as a single source provider of total jobsite solutions through our extensive product and service resources and technology offerings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The clear strategic direction of expanding product offerings and improving operational efficiency supports future growth.

factors.summary: Near-term catalysts include the continued success of Lean initiatives and the expansion of trench, power, and pump offerings.

evidence.text: The implementation of 'Lean' management techniques, including kaizen processes focused on continuous improvement. We have trained over 3,100 employees, over 70 percent of our district managers and approximately 55 percent of our branch managers on the Lean kaizen process. We continue to implement this program across our branch network, with the objectives of: reducing the cycle time associated with renting our equipment to customers; improving invoice accuracy and service quality; reducing the elapsed time for equipment pickup and delivery; and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of our repair and maintenance operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The success of Lean initiatives and product expansions serve as positive catalysts for near-term growth.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include scale, purchasing power, and a broad range of equipment and services.

evidence.text: These include a fleet of rental equipment with a total original equipment cost ('OEC') of $10.3 billion, and a national branch network that operates in 49 U.S. states and every Canadian province, and serves 99 of the largest 100 metropolitan areas in the United States. In addition, our size gives us greater purchasing power, the ability to provide customers with a broader range of equipment and services, the ability to provide customers with equipment that is more consistently well-maintained and therefore more productive and reliable, and the ability to enhance the earning potential of our assets by transferring equipment among branches to satisfy customer needs.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's scale, purchasing power, and broad equipment range provide significant competitive advantages.

factors.summary: The company is strengthening its balance sheet through debt management.

evidence.text: Since January 1, 2016, we have taken the following actions to improve our financial flexibility and liquidity, and to position us to invest the necessary capital in our business: Redeemed all of our 8 1/4 percent Senior Notes and 7 3/8 percent Senior Notes; Redeemed $1.1 billion principal amount of our 7 5/8 percent Senior Notes due 2022; Issued $1.0 billion principal amount of 5 7/8 percent Senior Notes due 2026; Issued $1.0 billion principal amount of 5 1/2 percent Senior Notes due 2027; Amended and extended our ABL facility; and Amended and extended our accounts receivable securitization facility.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Debt management actions, such as redeeming high-interest notes and issuing lower ones, improve financial flexibility.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, with available liquidity of $1.04 billion as of June 30, 2017.

evidence.text: As of June 30, 2017, we had available liquidity of $1.04 billion, including cash and cash equivalents of $338.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable free cash flow generation with $1.04 billion liquidity supports financial health.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is creating value through the NES acquisition, which is being successfully integrated.

evidence.text: For the six months ended June 30, 2017, equipment rental revenue increased 9.1 percent as compared to the same period in 2016, primarily reflecting a 12.4 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent, which includes the impact of the NES acquisition discussed in note 2 to the condensed consolidated financial statements, partially offset by a 1.2 percent rental rate decrease.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The successful integration of the NES acquisition drives revenue growth and value creation.
